name: node-require-directory
title: node-require-directory
summary: require all files in a directory
description:
require all files in a directory
Recursively iterates over specified directory, require()'ing each file, and
returning a nested hash structure containing those modules.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-require-directory because require all files in a directory.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-require-directory as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-require-directory with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-require-directory --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
